contract.title: Will the CLARITY Act be signed into law in 2026?
contract.resolution_criteria:
This market will resolve to 'Yes' if the Digital Asset Market Clarity Act of 2025
(H.R.3633) is passed by both chambers of the U.S. Congress and signed into law by
December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to 'No'.

The primary resolution source will be official information from Congress.gov, the
White House, and the U.S. Government Publishing Office. A consensus of credible
reporting will also be used.

The CLARITY Act (also referred to as the Digital Asset Market Clarity Act) establishes
a regulatory framework distinguishing digital commodities from digital securities,
granting the CFTC primary jurisdiction over digital commodity spot markets and
clarifying the SEC's jurisdiction over digital asset securities.

conditions.label: H.R.3633 passes both chambers and is signed
conditions.summary: The Digital Asset Market Clarity Act of 2025 (H.R.3633) passes the U.S. House and Senate and is signed into law by the President by Dec 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

contract.narrative: The CFTC’s May suit against Minnesota to block a state crypto law signals regulators can handle jurisdictional clashes, easing near-term pressure to advance the CLARITY Act. Other CFTC moves (committee changes, rebates, market-maker programs, trading-hour tweaks) are routine and largely non-directional. Passage by Congress and a 2026 presidential signature remain pending.

timeline.title: CFTC Sues Minnesota to Block State Law
timeline.one_line_why: CFTC suing Minnesota to block state crypto law suggests federal enforcement can resolve jurisdictional disputes, reducing pressure to enact CLARITY Act.

timeline.title: CDNA - Amendments to rules on Insider definition, jurisdiction… - 05/06/2026
timeline.one_line_why: Commodity Futures Trading Commission — Commodity Futures Trading Commission: tangential to resolution, no directional effect.

timeline.title: 05.01.2026 - Restructure the Disciplinary Panel and Appeals Committee from standing committees to ad hoc committees convened on an as-needed basis. - GMNI
timeline.one_line_why: Internal CFTC committee restructuring is administrative and unlikely to affect congressional passage or presidential signing of H.R.3633.

timeline.title: 05.01.2026 - Block Trade Rebate Program - KEX
timeline.one_line_why: CFTC's block-trade rebate action for Kalshi is an administrative market move unrelated to passage of H.R.3633, so it gives no clear signal.

timeline.title: COIN | Non-Crypto Market Maker Program - 04/16/2026
timeline.one_line_why: CFTC's Coinbase market-maker program shows regulator activity on crypto markets but doesn't materially change likelihood of H.R.3633 passage.

timeline.title: COIN | Modifications to trading hours… - 04/16/2026
timeline.one_line_why: Routine CFTC action on Coinbase trading hours is background regulatory activity and does not materially change odds that H.R.3633 will be enacted.

primary_drivers: Large influx of new high-severity incidents
Lack of recent mitigation or closure actions
heat_panel.explainer: Critical heat is elevated this week due to a sudden surge of unresolved high-severity findings and new incidents, with insufficient mitigation activity producing a large unaddressed risk spike.
